subject: Re: Fwd: GII NDTs / Not Good
body:
repair
--- LVJET@aol.com wrote:

> Jeffrey,
> Spoke to Bob Rockwood this morning, he will consult
> with Sean on the sale of 
> the G2 when he returns next week.
> Bob recommends painting our G2 white with a standard
> stripe, he said that 
> would be the best $100,000.00 you would spend. 
> First impressions are everything 
> on perspective buyers of older Gulfsteams according
> to Bob.
> Paint slots have a 4 month lead time, we have one
> reserved for June 16th. 
> Please advise if you agree? Interior is fine for
> re-sale.
> More corrosion found:
> All X-rays are complete, 1/4 inch deep corrosion was
> found on the R/H clothes 
> pin fitting, (this is the point that attaches the
> wing to the Fuselage).
> Repair kit available, cost $45,000.00 kit / labor
> $20,000.00 total $65,000.00 
> for starters. The corrosion on aft spar is estimated
> $25,000.00 plus 
> engineering cost.
> Bob is forwarding G4SP's for sale, Buyers market on
> G4's
> Bob's number is 540-554-8113 working from home,
> Office 540-665-5134
> Larry V.
> > From: JETCARE@aol.com
> Date: Fri, 12 May 2006 13:25:09 EDT
> Subject: GII NDTs / Not Good
> To: LVJET@aol.com
> CC: Dnrjet@aol.com, bill@bhjets.com,
> John.Davis@privatesky.net
> 
> Larry,
>     I just got off of the phone with John Davis (DOM
> Private Sky Aviation).  
> The reports are in for the NDTs and X-rays.  The Aft
> Right Hand clothes pin 
> fitting (452 B.L.33) has corrosion under the wing
> plank. The area involved is 
> approximately . 250" deep, and .500" long.  This is
> not good.  We have two 
> recourses, one is to approach GAC for a continued
> service letter with a reduced 
> inspection frequency, or for us to repair.  GAC may
> or may not grant the 
> continued service do to the depth of the corrosion,
> and if they did, they could 
> possibly ground the airplane at the next inspection
> interval if they see fit.  Two 
> repairs exist. One is to demate the wing from the
> fuselage and rework the plank 
> and replace the fitting (Private Sky has never
> lifted the fuselage from the 
> wing, and even GAC prefers option two). Option two
> installs a three piece 
> fitting per an ASC (all engineering is approved per
> the ASC eliminating further GAC 
> involvement).  The ASC kit is approximately 45k,
> with an additional 20K in 
> labor. If we decide which direction to go quickly,
> and kits are available this 
> will add 10 days to the return to service.  I would
> highly recommend that 
> Bristol be made aware of this issue and, be involved
> with our decision, because 
> this will most certainly become a contingency of
> sale.
>     Looking from a buyers perspective, I would not
> touch an airplane 
> operating under a continued service letter, nor
> would I accept a reduced price to 
> cover a repair that may run over do to unforeseen
> circumstances.
> This is now the hot item, and will be the driving
> factor of the project and 
> possible sale.  I will call you to discuss.  Please
> let me know which direction 
> to pursue.
> Larry M.
> 


__________________________________________________

body_html:
repair
--- LVJET@aol.com wrote:

Jeffrey,
Spoke to Bob Rockwood this morning, he will consult
with Sean on the sale of
the G2 when he returns next week.
Bob recommends painting our G2 white with a standard
stripe, he said that
would be the best $100,000.00 you would spend.
First impressions are everything
on perspective buyers of older Gulfsteams according
to Bob.
Paint slots have a 4 month lead time, we have one
reserved for June 16th.
Please advise if you agree? Interior is fine for
re-sale.
More corrosion found:
All X-rays are complete, 1/4 inch deep corrosion was
found on the R/H clothes
pin fitting, (this is the point that attaches the
wing to the Fuselage).
Repair kit available, cost $45,000.00 kit / labor
$20,000.00 total $65,000.00
for starters. The corrosion on aft spar is estimated
$25,000.00 plus
engineering cost.
Bob is forwarding G4SP's for sale, Buyers market on
G4's
Bob's number is 540-554-8113 working from home,
Office 540-665-5134
Larry V.

From: JETCARE@aol.com
Date: Fri, 12 May 2006 13:25:09 EDT
Subject: GII NDTs / Not Good
To: LVJET@aol.com
CC: Dnrjet@aol.com, bill@bhjets.com,
John.Davis@privatesky.net

Larry,
I just got off of the phone with John Davis (DOM
Private Sky Aviation).
The reports are in for the NDTs and X-rays. The Aft
Right Hand clothes pin
fitting (452 B.L.33) has corrosion under the wing
plank. The area involved is
approximately . 250" deep, and .500" long. This is
not good. We have two
recourses, one is to approach GAC for a continued
service letter with a reduced
inspection frequency, or for us to repair. GAC may
or may not grant the
continued service do to the depth of the corrosion,
and if they did, they could
possibly ground the airplane at the next inspection
interval if they see fit. Two
repairs exist. One is to demate the wing from the
fuselage and rework the plank
and replace the fitting (Private Sky has never
lifted the fuselage from the
wing, and even GAC prefers option two). Option two
installs a three piece
fitting per an ASC (all engineering is approved per
the ASC eliminating further GAC
involvement). The ASC kit is approximately 45k,
with an additional 20K in
labor. If we decide which direction to go quickly,
and kits are available this
will add 10 days to the return to service. I would
highly recommend that
Bristol be made aware of this issue and, be involved
with our decision, because
this will most certainly become a contingency of
sale.
Looking from a buyers perspective, I would not
touch an airplane
operating under a continued service letter, nor
would I accept a reduced price to
cover a repair that may run over do to unforeseen
circumstances.
This is now the hot item, and will be the driving
factor of the project and
possible sale. I will call you to discuss. Please
let me know which direction
to pursue.
Larry M.

Do You Yahoo!?
